page: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture using Neoverse-V1 cores, this instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU features a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The instance achieved a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 20513.0 and a software compilation score of 59595.0. Memory performance is a key strength, with a top-tier Passmark memory score of 3153.01 and uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 253.34 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is rated strong with a stress-ng multi-core score of 97897.68. For database and key-value workloads, the system demonstrated strong performance, executing 101.19 million Redis SET operations per second and scoring 31217.56 in Passmark database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth per unit of resource consumption compared to traditional x86 architectures. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-intensive operations. The instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, 512.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage, requiring network-attached volumes. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score of 3153.01 and uncached read memory bandwidth of 253.34 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving a Geekbench multi-core score of 20513.0 and executing 101.19 million Redis SET operations per second. The high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core makes this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and compilation workloads.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory performance with 253.34 GB/sec uncached read bandwidth
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, software compilation, and web serving
